# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), an agent service (harness dispatch
with profile-first routing), a persona system (bindable AI
personalities), a library registry (plugins with prompts, templates,
personas), and git-aware synchronization. This skill makes any
skills-compatible coding agent (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini
CLI, etc.) understand and operate the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of open beads. The *ready queue* is the subset
  with all dependencies closed. `ddx bead ready`, `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — a bead with at least one unclosed dependency.
  `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` (alias for
  `ddx agent execute-loop`); see `reference/work.md`.
- **Execute-bead** — the primitive: run one agent on one bead in an
  isolated worktree. `ddx agent execute-bead <id>`. The loop wraps
  this.
- **Execute-loop** — the queue-drain orchestrator; picks ready beads
  one at a time, calls execute-bead, closes on success, unclaims on
  failure. `ddx agent execute-loop` (or `ddx work`).
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010); broader
  than execute-bead. Includes execution definitions, execution
  records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/executions/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — the abstraction DDx uses to dispatch an agent
  (subprocess for claude/codex/gemini; embedded for the built-in
  `agent` harness). `ddx agent list`, `ddx agent capabilities`.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx agent run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Profile** — intent-based routing policy for `ddx agent run`:
  `cheap` (low-cost), `fast` (low-latency), `smart` (balanced
  default). `--profile cheap|fast|smart`.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx's `ddx agent run`
  (especially with `--quorum`) is the portable primitive; how a
  harness wraps that in a subagent is harness business. This skill
  does not take a position on subagent orchestration.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Quorum review** (`ddx agent run
  --quorum=<policy> --harnesses=<list>`) dispatches a review prompt
  across multiple harnesses and aggregates. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "run work", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harnesses, profiles, models, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve execute-bead commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx agent execute-bead` commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. Execute-bead does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Profile-first agent dispatch.** Default to
  `ddx agent run --profile smart` (or `cheap`/`fast` for specific
  intents). Only override with `--harness`/`--model`/`--effort`
  when pinning for a controlled test or known provider bug.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

DDx has two distinct review concepts. They are **not interchangeable**.

| Concept | Command | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| **Bead review** | `ddx bead review <id>` | Grades a bead's implementation against its declared acceptance criteria. Per-AC verdict with evidence. |
| **Quorum review** | `ddx agent run --quorum=<policy> --harnesses=<list>` | Dispatches a review prompt across multiple harnesses and aggregates the verdicts. Used for high-stakes or adversarial checks. |

## Bead review

```bash
ddx bead review <bead-id>
```

Generates a review-ready prompt that includes:

- Bead title, description, acceptance criteria (verbatim).
- Governing artifact content (follows `spec-id`).
- The diff since the bead's base commit (execution evidence commits
  excluded — see the related bug ddx-39e27896 for exclusion
  pathspec).
- Review instructions: output contract, verdict taxonomy.

The reviewer (whoever receives the prompt — a human, a harness via
`ddx agent run`, or a quorum) produces a structured verdict:

```
## Bead review: ddx-<id>

### Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK

### Per-criterion

1. [AC text]
   Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK
   Evidence: <file:line, test output, diff hunk, or explicit "not verifiable">

2. [AC text]
   ...

### Summary
<1-3 sentences of overall context>

### Findings
<detailed issues, if any; omit when verdict is APPROVE with no findings>
```

The review output can be stored as bead evidence:

```bash
ddx bead evidence add <id> --type review --body <path-to-review.md>
```

## Quorum review

```bash
ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=claude,codex,gemini \
  --prompt review-prompt.md
```

`--quorum=<policy>` values:

- `majority` — at least ⌈N/2⌉ harnesses must agree on the verdict.
- `unanimous` — all harnesses must agree.
- `any` — pass if any harness APPROVES (rarely useful; adversarial
  checks should require stronger consensus).

Use quorum when:

- The change is high-stakes (migrations, security surface, API
  contracts, auth).
- The review surface is controversial and one harness's opinion
  isn't enough.
- You want adversarial pressure against a single-model blindspot
  (different model families have different failure modes).

The aggregated output shows each harness's verdict side-by-side plus
the aggregate decision. Disagreements are surfaced, not hidden.

## Required evidence format

Both review kinds share an evidence contract: **no verdict without
evidence.**

- "APPROVE" with no `Evidence:` lines → reject as malformed.
- "REQUEST_CHANGES" without pointing at the change needed → reject.
- "BLOCK" without a reason → reject (see the related bug
  ddx-39e27896 about `review-malfunction` retries).

Evidence takes one of these forms:

1. **File:line reference**: `cli/internal/bead/store.go:142`
2. **Test output**: `FAIL: TestBeadClose_UnclaimsOnNonSuccess (0.01s)`
3. **Diff hunk**: a short quote from the diff showing the offending
   or approved change.
4. **Explicit "not verifiable"**: the AC is written in a way that
   cannot be mechanically checked; flag this as a bead-authoring
   issue rather than silently approving.

## Anti-patterns

- **Hallucinated approval.** "Looks good to me!" with no per-AC
  grading. The review is worthless.
- **Reviewing the prompt instead of the diff.** The reviewer evaluated
  "what you intended" not "what you actually changed." Verdict must
  reference the diff or the resulting code.
- **Verdict without evidence.** Every verdict must have at least one
  evidence line; APPROVE is no exception (evidence can be "AC met:
  `go test ./foo/... passes` output attached").
- **Silencing disagreement in quorum.** If two harnesses disagree,
  don't collapse to a single "decision." Surface the disagreement
  in the output and let the human decide.
- **Quorum for routine work.** Single-harness bead review is fine
  for most beads. Reserve quorum for the cases that justify the
  cost.

## Subagents: not this skill's business

Some harnesses can run a quorum in forked/isolated subagent
contexts; each harness implements this differently. This skill
doesn't specify how isolation is achieved —
`ddx agent run --quorum=<policy> --harnesses=<list>` is the
portable invocation, and the harness decides whether to fork,
spawn, or run inline.

## CLI reference

```bash
# Bead review (single-reviewer AC grading)
ddx bead review <id>                           # generate review prompt
ddx bead review <id> --execute --harness claude  # dispatch and grade
ddx bead evidence add <id> --type review --body r.md  # store review

# Quorum review (multi-harness)
ddx agent run --quorum=majority \
  --harnesses=claude,codex \
  --prompt review.md

ddx agent run --quorum=unanimous \
  --harnesses=claude,codex,gemini \
  --prompt review.md
```

Full flag list: `ddx bead review --help`, `ddx agent run --help`.

HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:139:{"acceptance":"1. Provider (or equivalent resolved-config struct) exposes a DetectedFlavor() accessor returning a string matching one of the known flavor values (or empty when indeterminate).\n\n2. The accessor reads from the cached probe result — no HTTP call on every invocation.\n\n3. If ProviderConfig.Flavor was set explicitly, DetectedFlavor() returns that value unchanged (caller-set wins over probe).\n\n4. Unit test: probe returns 'omlx' signal -\u003e Provider.DetectedFlavor() returns 'omlx'. ProviderConfig.Flavor='lmstudio' + probe says 'omlx' -\u003e DetectedFlavor() returns 'lmstudio'.\n\n5. CHANGELOG.md entry describing the new accessor.","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T01:40:17Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1635356","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:22:17.516353249Z","description":"## Context\n\nv0.3.11 introduced ProviderConfig.Flavor (lmstudio | omlx | openrouter | ollama) with probe-based autodetection when the field is absent. The probe hits /v1/models/status and /api/v0/models to figure out which flavor the endpoint speaks.\n\nDownstream consumers (DDx at DocumentDrivenDX/ddx, specifically the autorouting plan under ddx-2d974641 and the capability-gating bead ddx-4817edfd) need to know which flavor the library decided on for each configured provider — so their own routing/scoring/diagnostics can say 'this provider is omlx-flavor, so tool schemas go through auto-translation' without repeating the probe.\n\nToday this is not exposed via the public API. Downstream has two choices:\n1. Re-run the same probe independently. Wastes the library's work and can produce different answers if servers flap.\n2. Hard-code assumptions based on port numbers or URL patterns. Fragile — exactly the heuristic v0.3.11 replaced.\n\n## Ask\n\nExpose a getter on the resolved Provider (post-Load, post-detect) that returns the effective flavor:\n\n    // DetectedFlavor returns the flavor the runtime is using for this provider.\n    // If ProviderConfig.Flavor was set explicitly, returns that value. Otherwise\n    // returns the auto-detected flavor from the startup probe. Empty string when\n    // the probe was inconclusive or the provider is not a supported flavor-family.\n    func (p *Provider) DetectedFlavor() string\n\nCache the probe result so DetectedFlavor is a cheap lookup, not a fresh HTTP call.\n\n## Use case\n\nDDx uses this to:\n- Tag routing events with the effective flavor so debugging is self-contained in execution evidence.\n- Feed capability gating (supports_tools, supports_structured_output) off the flavor in scoring.\n- Generate human-readable 'ddx agent check --verbose' output showing each provider's flavor.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Exposing the RAW probe results (HTTP responses). Only the distilled flavor.\n- Making the flavor mutable at runtime. Providers are loaded + detected once per process.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T01:40:17.954331827Z","id":"agent-92f0f324","issue_type":"task","labels":["agent","kind:enhancement","area:provider","area:api"],"notes":"Fresh-eyes review 2026-04-18: Provider already has a flavor-adjacent field — providerSystem (URL-heuristic from openAIIdentity, stored at New() time). It's used on 4 hot paths: AttemptMetadata.ProviderSystem, openRouterCostAttribution, ProviderIdentity(), and streaming attempt metadata. Those fire every response and cannot afford a network probe. DetectedFlavor() must layer on top: return explicit Config.Flavor if set, otherwise cached probe result via sync.Once, otherwise fall back to providerSystem. Do NOT migrate the 4 hot callsites off providerSystem in this bead — that's a telemetry contract change (CONTRACT-001) and scope creep. Docstring must explicitly document the two-path layering: DetectedFlavor() for pre-dispatch gating (probe-confirmed); ProviderIdentity() for per-response telemetry (eager, non-blocking).","owner":"erik","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"expose Provider.DetectedFlavor() so downstream tools can introspect probe result without re-probing","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:57:30.690987048Z"}

HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:159:{"acceptance":"- [ ] CONTRACT-001-ddx-agent-service.md is the sole external-surface authority on the agent side\n- [ ] DdxAgent Service interface exposes 7 methods (Execute, TailSessionLog, ListHarnesses, ListProviders, ListModels, HealthCheck, ResolveRoute, RouteStatus) with the input/output types specified in the plan\n- [ ] All five subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, opencode, pi, gemini) are ported into agent internal/harnesses/ with behavioral parity tests\n- [ ] Native agent harness wins on at least one batch noninteractive task in the integration suite\n- [ ] All entanglement docs deleted (CONTRACT-002 in agent; SD-015, SD-015-resolution-path-trace, SD-023, plan-2026-04-08 in DDx)\n- [ ] Internal/ tree blocks external imports of compaction, prompt, tool, session, observations, modelcatalog, provider/* (compile-time enforcement)\n- [ ] DDx imports nothing from agent except the root agentlib package\n- [ ] Integration suite runs across all available harnesses with cost cap and determinism controls\n- [ ] All six \"badly implemented\" routing smells fixed structurally, not patched\n- [ ] agent v0.4.0-pre tagged (legacy + new contract); v0.5.0 tagged (legacy gone, internal/ enforced)","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:21:28.726590048Z","description":"Consolidate LLM routing + harness orchestration entirely into ddx-agent. The module becomes the one stop shop for optimally routed one-shot noninteractive agentic prompts. Two roles: (1) direct first-class agent over native providers (bragi, vidar, openrouter, anthropic), designed to win at batch noninteractive; (2) wrapper around subprocess agents (claude, codex, opencode, pi, gemini) for convenience and cost management.\n\nDDx becomes a thin Service consumer with no agent-internal imports. HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:245:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Options struct contains FakeProvider + 3 hooks\n- [ ] Build tag testseam guards each\n- [ ] Production build (no testseam tag) cannot compile code that constructs any of them\n- [ ] Each seam has at least one demonstration test in agent repo","closing_commit_sha":"2b089c69082f175b35dbfef683769b625b99c67a","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:22:35.128700018Z","description":"Implement the four test-injection seams from CONTRACT-001. All guarded by \"//go:build testseam\" so production binaries cannot construct them.\n\n1. FakeProvider — three patterns (Static script, Dynamic callback, InjectError per-call). Replaces the existing virtual.New/InlineResponse seam used in 32 DDx test sites today.\n2. PromptAssertionHook(systemPrompt, userPrompt, contextFiles) — observe constructed prompts.\n3. CompactionAssertionHook(messagesBefore, messagesAfter, tokensFreed) — observe real compactions only (no-ops never delivered).\n4. ToolWiringHook(harness, toolNames) — observe resolved tool list per Execute.\n\nWire all four into Options. Tests: each seam exercised by at least one unit test in the agent repo, demonstrating it captures what it should.","id":"agent-ef5a50d0","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:build","kind:testing","area:contract"],"parent":"agent-a770e781","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"service: implement four test seams (FakeProvider + PromptAssertionHook + CompactionAssertionHook + ToolWiringHook)","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:00:55.425934436Z"}

Add tests that:\n- prove `ddx-agent usage` supports every documented output mode, including CSV, while preserving known-cost vs unknown-cost semantics and time-window filtering\n- prove unwritable log directories or telemetry export failures do not abort a successful run, emit an operator-visible warning, and leave any partial artifacts readable\n- where appropriate, assert that correlation metadata survives through both session start and session end so replay/usage tools can attribute runs consistently\n\nRelevant files: `cmd/ddx-agent/usage_cli_test.go`, `session/logger_test.go`, `telemetry/telemetry_test.go`, and CLI/session integration helpers.","id":"agent-f2b590db","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:test","kind:testing","area:telemetry"],"notes":"Superseded by agent-010074fa after shell-quoting corruption in the first batch bead-creation pass.","parent":"agent-42d295a3","priority":1,"spec-id":"docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-005-logging-and-cost.md","status":"closed","title":"Add reporting and observability-resilience tests for usage output modes and best-effort failures","updated_at":"2026-04-10T15:56:08.736397107Z"}

HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:280:{"acceptance":"Streaming timing uses real delta arrival timestamps rather than subtracting cumulative callback time; a regression test with callback sleep plus inter-delta provider delay verifies ddx.timing.generation_ms still includes the provider delay and EventLLMDelta timestamps remain monotonic.","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T01:44:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3948379","closing_commit_sha":"863eecb98d4dcca506fdb42a6a67864410c38442","created_at":"2026-04-10T01:42:19.102557411Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: stream_consume.go:44\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: consumeStream now computes each delta timestamp as time.Now().Add(-callbackDelay) and grows callbackDelay by the duration of prior EventLLMDelta callbacks. When a later streamed delta becomes ready while an earlier callback is still running, this subtracts both observer latency and real provider generation time that elapsed during that callback. In the shipped providers, ChatStream uses a single-slot channel, so later deltas can queue behind the consumer and then be back-dated when read. That makes ddx.timing.generation_ms and delta event timestamps too small or even flat across distinct arrivals, which violates CONTRACT-001's requirement that timing reflect the streamed output window rather than an adjusted observer schedule.\nSuggested fix: Capture an arrival timestamp at the producer or carry it on StreamDelta so consumeStream can use actual delta arrival times. At minimum, add a regression test that combines callback sleep with real delayBetween timing and verifies generation_ms still includes the provider delay that elapsed while the callback was running.","id":"hx-1fcb3c84","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"stream_consume.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: callback-delay compensation undercounts real stream generation","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:45:22.728662476Z"}
HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:285:{"acceptance":"A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed provider-reported/configured cost rollups.","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T04:17:15Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"517258","closing_commit_sha":"c083df5f46fe37557b8feedf66af32013a0a9e35","created_at":"2026-04-10T04:02:07.463686182Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:289\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a provider-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.\nSuggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:314:{"acceptance":"If attempt cost provenance is unknown, llm.response and session.end do not emit a positive cost_usd derived from DefaultPricing; known provider-reported or configured costs still propagate; regression tests cover known-cost and unknown-cost cases.","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T22:06:44Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1912325","closing_commit_sha":"770742fb96448ee3e5ea05811884d95f9e90c7e1","created_at":"2026-04-09T21:51:41.640860953Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit de8c772.\nFile: loop.go:219, loop.go:241, provider/openai/openai.go:83, provider/anthropic/anthropic.go:90, pricing.go:12\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: This change introduces structured cost provenance on resp.Attempt.Cost and explicitly marks both providers as source=unknown, but the runtime still unconditionally computes cost_usd from DefaultPricing and emits it on every successful llm.response. That produces internally contradictory telemetry: the same event can say the attempt cost source is unknown while also publishing a numeric USD value from a static fallback table. CONTRACT-001 explicitly forbids generic stale pricing tables as a fallback and requires unknown cost to remain unknown when no reported or configured cost exists.\nSuggested fix: Stop deriving per-attempt and session cost from DefaultPricing once attempt-level cost provenance exists. Use provider-reported or configured runtime pricing only when available, otherwise omit numeric cost and keep the rollup unknown. Add tests that assert unknown-cost attempts do not emit positive cost_usd values.","id":"hx-5521ea3d","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"loop.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: llm.response cost_usd conflicts with unknown attempt cost provenance","updated_at":"2026-04-09T22:08:43.726212836Z"}

HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:336:{"acceptance":"CONTRACT-001 explicitly defines how the root invoke_agent span represents known session totals when child chat spans have mixed known provenance; the contract no longer requires an impossible or ambiguous ddx.cost.source/pricing_ref combination at the root; conformance tests or checklist coverage include a mixed provider-reported/configured session example.","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T04:23:38Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"547576","closing_commit_sha":"c083df5f46fe37557b8feedf66af32013a0a9e35","created_at":"2026-04-10T04:15:39.179486428Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md:221\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: CONTRACT-001 says the root invoke_agent span should emit a session cost rollup whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only become unknown when any child cost is unknown. But the contract never defines what ddx.cost.source, ddx.cost.currency, and ddx.cost.pricing_ref should look like when all child chat costs are known yet their provenance differs across turns, such as one provider-reported turn plus one configured-cost turn. That makes the required root shape ambiguous: ddx.cost.source is required when amount is known, but the allowed enum only models single-attempt provenance. The current implementation fell into this gap and downgraded a known total to unknown when provenance metadata differed.\nSuggested fix: Amend the root-span cost-rollup section to specify a conformant representation for mixed known-cost provenance. Either add an explicit aggregate/root rollup source shape, or state that the root may still emit ddx.cost.amount and currency while omitting or summarizing provenance fields that cannot be represented cleanly. Add a contract-focused regression test or checklist entry covering mixed provider-reported/configured sessions.","id":"hx-7853958b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics","updated_at":"2026-04-10T04:24:48.231802016Z"}

HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:345:{"acceptance":"Streaming chat spans measure ddx.timing.first_token_ms from the same timestamp used to start the chat span; a test with a blocking EventLLMRequest callback and a streaming provider confirms first_token_ms includes callback delay before ChatStream begins.","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T01:18:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3894095","closing_commit_sha":"4df0f7e92c3f685d9843714ab7b10e5a4a04741e","created_at":"2026-04-10T01:17:28.821747551Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:175\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 4df0f7e moved `streamStart` earlier inside `consumeStream`, but `ddx.timing.first_token_ms` is still measured after the chat span starts. `Run` starts the `chat` span at loop.go:175, then emits `EventLLMRequest` before calling `consumeStream`; because callbacks are user-supplied and can block, the exported attribute can still under-report first-token latency by the callback time and therefore violate CONTRACT-001's requirement that the value measure from span start to first streamed token.\nSuggested fix: Capture the chat span/request start timestamp in `Run` and thread it into `consumeStream` so `Attempt.Timing.FirstToken` is derived from the same timestamp that anchors the `chat` span. Add an end-to-end test where the request callback sleeps before streaming begins and assert the chat span's `ddx.timing.first_token_ms` includes that delay.","id":"hx-8bf6ee3a","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:lib"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"loop.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: first_token_ms still excludes pre-stream callback latency","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:19:57.86077717Z"}

HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:376:{"acceptance":"go test ./... passes in a clean worktree at HEAD; TestRun_CONTRACT001MixedKnownCostProvenance observes ddx.cost.amount on the root invoke_agent span for mixed known-cost sessions; root mixed-provenance rollups omit unstable provenance metadata instead of downgrading the whole session cost to unknown.","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T04:33:52Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"576954","closing_commit_sha":"421f59e695319df41a2ec9016b13f652cb98ed6e","created_at":"2026-04-10T04:32:06.30077824Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:696\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: high\nDescription: Commit 5f14aaa updates CONTRACT-001 and adds TestRun_CONTRACT001MixedKnownCostProvenance, but the runtime still emits root `invoke_agent` cost as unknown when child chat spans have mixed known provenance. In a clean worktree at commit 5f14aaa, `go test ./...` fails because telemetry_contract_test.go expects the root span to carry ddx.cost.amount=0.03, while a standalone reproduction shows the ended root span still exports only ddx.cost.source=unknown. That leaves the implementation out of sync with the amended contract and keeps the original telemetry bug unfixed.\nSuggested fix: Update the root-session cost rollup logic in loop.go so mixed known provenance preserves ddx.cost.amount while omitting only the unstable provenance fields (ddx.cost.source, ddx.cost.currency, ddx.cost.pricing_ref) as CONTRACT-001 now requires. Keep the new conformance test green with go test ./... in a clean worktree.","id":"hx-cacc76f6","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"loop.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: mixed-provenance root cost rollup still exports unknown","updated_at":"2026-04-10T04:34:13.786934256Z"}
HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:377:{"acceptance":"When any contributing attempt has unknown cost provenance, session.end omits cost_usd instead of emitting -1; replay/session parsing distinguishes omitted cost from zero-cost local cost; regression tests cover both known-cost and unknown-cost session rollups.","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T22:13:53Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1968732","closing_commit_sha":"0fd0aca909b39909db31ad74954f81e271c80174","created_at":"2026-04-09T22:12:30.075052671Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:360, loop_test.go:506, session/event.go:52, session/replay.go:108\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The last-commit fix removed DefaultPricing fallback from per-attempt telemetry, but the session rollup still serializes a numeric cost on every session.end event by copying result.CostUSD unconditionally. When any contributing attempt has unknown provenance, result.CostUSD becomes -1, so session.end now emits `cost_usd: -1` instead of omitting the amount. That still violates CONTRACT-001 and SD-001, which require unknown rollup cost amounts to be omitted. The new regression test in loop_test.go also codifies unconditional session.end cost emission, so a contract-compliant fix would currently fail tests. Downstream session consumers compound the issue because SessionEndData uses a plain float64 and Replay treats zero as `$0 (local)`, so omitted cost cannot be represented distinctly from free local execution.\nSuggested fix: Make session.end omit cost_usd when the run cost is unknown, update session event/replay types to distinguish omitted cost from zero-cost local runs, and replace the current session-end assertion with regression coverage for both known-cost and unknown-cost sessions.","id":"hx-cce583e1","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"loop.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: session.end still emits unknown cost as numeric sentinel","updated_at":"2026-04-09T22:14:59.058088662Z"}
HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:382:{"acceptance":"A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed provider-reported/configured cost rollups.","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T04:41:54Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"615913","closing_commit_sha":"421f59e695319df41a2ec9016b13f652cb98ed6e","created_at":"2026-04-10T04:22:39.474002339Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:289\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The last commit's root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That discards a valid session total even though every contributing chat span has known cost, which conflicts with FEAT-005 requirement 22, ADR-001's known-vs-unknown cost policy, and CONTRACT-001's rule that the root rollup only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. In the committed code, a two-turn session with one provider-reported attempt and one exact configured-cost attempt yields result.CostUSD=0.03 while applySessionCostAttributes still emits root ddx.cost.source=unknown because sessionCostStable became false.\nSuggested fix: Preserve root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. HEAD:.ddx/beads.jsonl:405:{"acceptance":"Streaming timing uses pre-callback delta arrival timestamps for ddx.timing.first_token_ms and ddx.timing.generation_ms; a test with a blocking EventLLMDelta callback confirms the timing values do not grow by callback sleep time.","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T01:36:44Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3936062","closing_commit_sha":"863eecb98d4dcca506fdb42a6a67864410c38442","created_at":"2026-04-10T01:24:20.154769542Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: stream_consume.go:41\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new anchor fixes pre-stream request callback latency, but consumeStream still timestamps the first output only after emitting EventLLMDelta. Because emitCallback is user-supplied and can block, a slow llm.delta callback will still inflate ddx.timing.first_token_ms and ddx.timing.generation_ms, which violates CONTRACT-001s requirement that timing reflect span-to-token and generation windows rather than observer latency.\nSuggested fix: Capture the arrival timestamp for each delta before invoking the callback, use that pre-callback timestamp for the first output boundary and generation window math, and add a regression test where an EventLLMDelta callback sleeps while the first streamed delta arrives.","id":"hx-fd626e74","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: first_token_ms still counts blocking llm.delta callbacks","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:38:50.580711052Z"}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_22","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "git show HEAD:docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md | nl -ba | sed -n '1,320p'"","aggregated_output":"     1	# CONTRACT-003: DdxAgent Service Interface
     2	
     3	**Status:** Draft
     4	**Owner:** DDX Agent maintainers
     5	**Replaces:** CONTRACT-002-ddx-harness-interface (deleted; entanglement-era contract)
     6	
     7	## Purpose
     8	
     9	This contract defines the **entire public Go surface** of the `ddx-agent` module.
    10	Anything not reachable through the `DdxAgent` interface — or through the input/output
    11	struct types referenced by its methods — is internal and may change without notice.
    12	
    13	Consumers (DDx CLI, future HELIX/Dun integrations, the standalone `ddx-agent`
    14	binary, anything else) interact only through this surface. **They do not import
    15	agent internal packages.** When new behavior is needed, consumers file an issue or
    16	PR against this contract; agent maintainers decide whether the surface grows.
    17	
    18	## Module value proposition
    19	
    20	`ddx-agent` is the one stop shop for optimally routed one-shot noninteractive
    21	agentic prompts. Two roles:
    22	
    23	1. **Direct first-class agent** over native model providers (LM Studio, OpenRouter,
    24	   Anthropic, etc.). Designed to be the high-performance choice for batch
    25	   noninteractive tasks.
    26	2. **Wrapper around other agents** — subprocess harnesses (claude, codex,
    27	   opencode, pi, gemini) — used when their interactive features, vendor billing,
    28	   specific capabilities, or comparison/fallback routing wants them in the
    29	   candidate pool.
    30	
    31	A single internal routing engine ranks `(harness, provider?, model)` candidates
    32	uniformly. Consumers see one surface; the internals decide how to dispatch.
    33	
    34	## Interface
    35	
    36	```go
    37	package agentlib
    38	
    39	import (
    40	    "context"
    41	    "io"
    42	    "time"
    43	    "encoding/json"
    44	)
    45	
    46	// DdxAgent is the entire public Go surface of the ddx-agent module.
    47	type DdxAgent interface {
    48	    // Execute runs an agent task in-process; emits Events on the channel until
    49	    // the task terminates (channel closes). The final event (type=final) carries
    50	    // status, usage, cost, session log path, optional message history, and
    51	    // routing_actual (the resolved fallback chain that fired).
    52	    Execute(ctx context.Context, req ExecuteRequest) (<-chan Event, error)
    53	
    54	    // TailSessionLog streams events from a previously-started or in-progress
    55	    // session by ID. Used by clients (DDx workers, UIs) to subscribe to a run
    56	    // started elsewhere — e.g., a server-managed worker that the CLI wants to
    57	    // follow. Multi-subscriber-safe.
    58	    TailSessionLog(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (<-chan Event, error)
    59	
    60	    // ListHarnesses returns metadata for every registered harness (native and
    61	    // subprocess). HarnessInfo includes install state, supported permission
    62	    // levels, supported reasoning values, and live quota when applicable.
    63	    ListHarnesses(ctx context.Context) ([]HarnessInfo, error)
    64	
    65	    // ListProviders returns providers known to the native-agent harness with
    66	    // live status, configured-default markers, and cooldown state.
    67	    ListProviders(ctx context.Context) ([]ProviderInfo, error)
    68	
    69	    // ListModels returns models matching the filter, with full metadata
    70	    // (cost, perf signals, capabilities, context length, ranking).
    71	    ListModels(ctx context.Context, filter ModelFilter) ([]ModelInfo, error)
    72	
    73	    // HealthCheck triggers a fresh probe and updates internal state.
    74	    // Target.Type is "harness" or "provider".
    75	    HealthCheck(ctx context.Context, target HealthTarget) error
    76	
    77	    // ResolveRoute resolves a single under-specified request to a concrete
    78	    // (Harness, Provider, Model). The returned RouteDecision can be passed
    79	    // back to Execute via ExecuteRequest.PreResolved to skip re-resolution
    80	    // (used by dry-run-then-execute flows).
    81	    ResolveRoute(ctx context.Context, req RouteRequest) (*RouteDecision, error)
    82	
    83	    // RouteStatus returns global routing state across all routes: cooldowns,
    84	    // recent decisions, observation-derived per-(provider,model) latency.
    85	    // Distinct from per-request ResolveRoute — this is the read-only operator
    86	    // dashboard view.
    87	    RouteStatus(ctx context.Context) (*RouteStatusReport, error)
    88	}
    89	
    90	// New constructs a DdxAgent. Options is intentionally minimal.
    91	func New(opts Options) (DdxAgent, error)
    92	```
    93	
    94	**Eight methods total.** `Execute` is the primary verb; `TailSessionLog`,
    95	`ListHarnesses`, `ListProviders`, `ListModels`, `HealthCheck`, `ResolveRoute`,
    96	`RouteStatus` are the supporting surface. (Counted as "7 + New" in earlier
    97	drafts; interface has 8 methods.)
    98	
    99	## Public types
   100	
   101	```go
   102	type Options struct {
   103	    ConfigPath string    // optional override; default $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ddx-agent/config.yaml
   104	    Logger     io.Writer // optional; agent writes structured session logs internally regardless
   105	
   106	    // Test-only injection seams. Each MUST be nil in production builds —
   107	    // enforced by build tag `//go:build testseam`. Forming an Options with
   108	    // any of these set in a non-test build is a compile error. Four seams
   109	    // exist because consumers today inject at four different layers.
   110	    FakeProvider            *FakeProvider
   111	    PromptAssertionHook     PromptAssertionHook
   112	    CompactionAssertionHook CompactionAssertionHook
   113	    ToolWiringHook          ToolWiringHook
   114	}
   115	
   116	// Reasoning is the single public model-reasoning control. It is one scalar:
   117	// named values such as auto/off/low/medium/high/minimal/xhigh/max, or numeric
   118	// strings produced by ReasoningTokens for explicit token budgets.
   119	//
   120	// The root package may re-export this type, constants, and helper from a shared
   121	// leaf package such as internal/reasoning. Internal packages such as
   122	// internal/modelcatalog import the leaf package, not root agent, to avoid
   123	// root-agent/internal-modelcatalog import cycles.
   124	type Reasoning string
   125	
   126	const (
   127	    ReasoningAuto   Reasoning = "auto"
   128	    ReasoningOff    Reasoning = "off"
   129	    ReasoningLow    Reasoning = "low"
   130	    ReasoningMedium Reasoning = "medium"
   131	    ReasoningHigh   Reasoning = "high"
   132	    ReasoningMinimal Reasoning = "minimal" // accepted only when advertised
   133	    ReasoningXHigh  Reasoning = "xhigh"    // normalizes x-high
   134	    ReasoningMax    Reasoning = "max"      // requires known model/provider max
   135	)
   136	
   137	func ReasoningTokens(n int) Reasoning
   138	
   139	type ExecuteRequest struct {
   140	    Prompt       string  // required
   141	    SystemPrompt string  // optional; agent supplies a sane default if empty
   142	    Model        string  // optional; resolved via ResolveRoute if empty
   143	    Provider     string  // optional preference (soft); empty = router decides
   144	    Harness      string  // optional preference (hard); empty = router decides
   145	    ModelRef     string  // optional alias from the catalog: cheap/standard/smart/<custom>
   146	    Temperature  float32 // model sampling temperature; 0 = deterministic
   147	    Seed         int64   // sampling seed; 0 = unset/provider chooses
   148	    Reasoning    Reasoning // optional; auto|off|low|medium|high|minimal|xhigh|max|<tokens>
   149	    Permissions  string  // "safe" | "supervised" | "unrestricted"; default "safe"
   150	    WorkDir      string  // required when the chosen harness uses tools
   151	
   152	    // PreResolved bypasses ResolveRoute when the caller already has a decision
   153	    // (e.g., from a prior ResolveRoute call). When non-nil, agent uses these
   154	    // values verbatim and does not re-route. Provider/Model/Harness fields
   155	    // above are ignored in this mode.
   156	    PreResolved *RouteDecision
   157	
   158	    // Three independent timeout knobs:
   159	    //   Timeout         — wall-clock cap; the request fails after this duration
   160	    //                     regardless of activity. 0 = no cap.
   161	    //   IdleTimeout     — streaming-quiet cap; the request fails after this
   162	    //                     duration of no events from the model. 0 = use harness
   163	    //                     default (typically 60s).
   164	    //   ProviderTimeout — per-HTTP-request cap to the provider; longer requests
   165	    //                     are retried per the harness's failover rules. 0 = use
   166	    //                     provider default.
   167	    Timeout         time.Duration
   168	    IdleTimeout     time.Duration
   169	    ProviderTimeout time.Duration
   170	
   171	    // Optional stall policy. When non-nil, agent enforces and ends execution
   172	    // with Status="stalled" if any limit hits. The agent also derives an
   173	    // implicit MaxIterations ceiling from StallPolicy (typically 2× the
   174	    // ReadOnly limit) — caller does not configure MaxIterations directly.
   175	    StallPolicy *StallPolicy
   176	
   177	    // SessionLogDir overrides the default session-log directory for this
   178	    // request. Used by execute-bead to direct logs into a per-bundle evidence
   179	    // directory. Empty = use Options.ConfigPath-derived default.
   180	    SessionLogDir string
   181	
   182	    // Metadata is bidirectional: echoed back in every Event via Event.Metadata,
   183	    // AND stamped onto every line written to the session log (e.g., bead_id,
   184	    // attempt_id) so external log consumers can correlate.
   185	    Metadata map[string]string
   186	}
   187	
   188	type StallPolicy struct {
   189	    MaxReadOnlyToolIterations int // 0 = disabled
   190	    MaxNoopCompactions        int // 0 = disabled
   191	}
   192	
   193	type RouteRequest struct {
   194	    Model       string
   195	    Provider    string
   196	    Harness     string

   198	    Reasoning   Reasoning
   199	    Permissions string
   200	}
   201

    15	// DdxAgent is the entire public Go surface of the ddx-agent module.
    16	// See CONTRACT-003 for the full specification.
    17	type DdxAgent interface {
    18		Execute(ctx context.Context, req ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan ServiceEvent, error)
    19		TailSessionLog(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (<-chan ServiceEvent, error)
    20		ListHarnesses(ctx context.Context) ([]HarnessInfo, error)
    21		ListProviders(ctx context.Context) ([]ProviderInfo, error)
    22		ListModels(ctx context.Context, filter ModelFilter) ([]ModelInfo, error)
    23		HealthCheck(ctx context.Context, target HealthTarget) error
    24		ResolveRoute(ctx context.Context, req RouteRequest) (*RouteDecision, error)
    25		RouteStatus(ctx context.Context) (*RouteStatusReport, error)
    26	}
    27	
    28	// ServiceConfig provides provider and routing data to the service without
    29	// creating an import cycle from the root package into agent/config.
    30	// Callers wrap their loaded *config.Config in a type that satisfies this interface.
    31	type ServiceConfig interface {
    32		// ProviderNames returns provider names in stable order (default first).
    33		ProviderNames() []string
    34		// DefaultProviderName returns the name of the configured default provider.
    35		DefaultProviderName() string
    36		// Provider returns the raw config values for a named provider.
    37		Provider(name string) (ServiceProviderEntry, bool)
    38		// ModelRouteNames returns configured model-route names.
    39		ModelRouteNames() []string
    40		// ModelRouteCandidates returns the provider names referenced by a route.
    41		ModelRouteCandidates(routeName string) []string
    42		// ModelRouteConfig returns the full route config for a named route.
    43		// Returns zero value when the route is not found.
    44		ModelRouteConfig(routeName string) ServiceModelRouteConfig
    45		// HealthCooldown returns the configured cooldown duration (0 = use default 30s).
    46		HealthCooldown() time.Duration
    47		// WorkDir is the base directory for file-backed health state.
    48		WorkDir() string
    49	}

   302	// loadServiceConfig, when non-nil, is called by New to load a ServiceConfig
   303	// from a directory path when opts.ServiceConfig is nil. It is registered by
   304	// the config package via init() to break the import cycle (config imports root).
   305	var loadServiceConfig func(dir string) (ServiceConfig, error)
   306	
   307	// RegisterConfigLoader is called by the config package's init() to install the
   308	// config-loading function. Do not call this from application code.
   309	func RegisterConfigLoader(fn func(dir string) (ServiceConfig, error)) {
   310		loadServiceConfig = fn

   312	
   313	// New constructs a DdxAgent. When opts.ServiceConfig is nil, New attempts to
   314	// load configuration automatically:
   315	//  1. If opts.ConfigPath is non-empty, load from filepath.Dir(opts.ConfigPath).
   316	//  2. Otherwise, load from the default global config location.
   317	//
   318	// Automatic loading requires the config package to be imported somewhere in the

   323		if opts.ServiceConfig == nil && loadServiceConfig != nil && shouldAutoLoadServiceConfig(opts) {

   325			if opts.ConfigPath != "" {
   326				dir = filepath.Dir(opts.ConfigPath)
   327			}

   351	func supportedPermissions(cfg harnesses.HarnessConfig) []string {

CONSTANTS

const (
	ReasoningAuto    = reasoning.ReasoningAuto
	ReasoningOff     = reasoning.ReasoningOff
	ReasoningLow     = reasoning.ReasoningLow
	ReasoningMedium  = reasoning.ReasoningMedium
	ReasoningHigh    = reasoning.ReasoningHigh

FUNCTIONS

func RegisterConfigLoader(fn func(dir string) (ServiceConfig, error))
    RegisterConfigLoader is called by the config package's init() to install the
    config-loading function. Do not call this from application code.


TYPES

type CooldownState struct {
	Reason    string    // "consecutive_failures" | "manual" | etc.
	Until     time.Time // when the cooldown expires
	FailCount int       // number of consecutive failures that triggered the cooldown
	LastError string    // last recorded error message, if available
}
    CooldownState describes an active routing cooldown for a provider.

type CostInfo struct {
	InputPerMTok  float64 // USD per 1M input tokens; 0 = unknown/free
	OutputPerMTok float64 // USD per 1M output tokens; 0 = unknown/free
}
    CostInfo holds per-token cost metadata for a model.

type DdxAgent interface {
	Execute(ctx context.Context, req ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan ServiceEvent, error)
	TailSessionLog(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (<-chan ServiceEvent, error)
	ListHarnesses(ctx context.Context) ([]HarnessInfo, error)
	ListProviders(ctx context.Context) ([]ProviderInfo, error)
	ListModels(ctx context.Context, filter ModelFilter) ([]ModelInfo, error)
	HealthCheck(ctx context.Context, target HealthTarget) error
	ResolveRoute(ctx context.Context, req RouteRequest) (*RouteDecision, error)
	RouteStatus(ctx context.Context) (*RouteStatusReport, error)
}
    DdxAgent is the entire public Go surface of the ddx-agent module. See
    CONTRACT-003 for the full specification.

func New(opts ServiceOptions) (DdxAgent, error)
    New constructs a DdxAgent. When opts.ServiceConfig is nil, New attempts to
    load configuration automatically:
     1. If opts.ConfigPath is non-empty, load from
        filepath.Dir(opts.ConfigPath).
     2. Otherwise, load from the default global config location.

    Automatic loading requires the config package to be imported somewhere in
    the binary (it registers the loader via init). If the config package is
    not imported and ServiceConfig is nil, the service starts without provider
    config (ListProviders/HealthCheck will return errors until config is
    injected).

type HarnessInfo struct {
	Name                 string
	Type                 string // "native" | "subprocess"
	Available            bool
	Path                 string
	Error                string
	IsLocal              bool
	IsSubscription       bool
	ExactPinSupport      bool
	DefaultModel         string   // built-in default model when no override is supplied
	SupportedPermissions []string // subset of {"safe","supervised","unrestricted"}
	SupportedReasoning   []string // values such as {"low","medium","high","xhigh","max"}
	CostClass            string   // "local" | "cheap" | "medium" | "expensive"
	Quota                *QuotaState
}
    HarnessInfo describes a registered harness as defined in CONTRACT-003.

type HealthTarget struct {
	Type string // "harness" | "provider"
	Name string
}
    HealthTarget identifies what to health-check.

type ModelFilter struct {
	Harness  string
	Provider string
}
    ModelFilter filters ListModels results.

type ModelInfo struct {
	ID            string
	Provider      string
	Harness       string
	ContextLength int
	Capabilities  []string
	Cost          CostInfo
	PerfSignal    PerfSignal
	Available     bool
	IsConfigured  bool   // matches an explicit model_routes entry
	IsDefault     bool   // matches the configured default model
	CatalogRef    string // canonical catalog reference if recognized
	RankPosition  int    // ordinal in latest discovery rank; -1 if unranked
}
    ModelInfo describes a model with full metadata per CONTRACT-003.

type PerfSignal struct {
	SpeedTokensPerSec float64 // 0 = unknown
	SWEBenchVerified  float64 // 0 = unknown
}
    PerfSignal holds observed performance data for a model.

type ProviderInfo struct {
	Name          string
	Type          string // "openai" | "openrouter" | "lmstudio" | "omlx" | "ollama" | "anthropic" | "virtual"
	BaseURL       string
	Endpoints     []ServiceProviderEndpoint
	Status        string // "connected" | "unreachable" | "error: <msg>"
	ModelCount    int
	Capabilities  []string       // e.g. {"tool_use","streaming","json_mode"}
	IsDefault     bool           // matches the configured default_provider
	DefaultModel  string         // per-provider configured default model, if any
	CooldownState *CooldownState // nil if not in cooldown
}
    ProviderInfo describes a provider with live status per CONTRACT-003.

type QuotaState struct {
	Windows    []harnesses.QuotaWindow `json:"windows"`
	CapturedAt time.Time               `json:"captured_at"`
	Fresh      bool                    `json:"fresh"`
}
    QuotaState is a live quota snapshot for a harness. Nil means not applicable.

type Reasoning = reasoning.Reasoning

func ReasoningTokens(n int) Reasoning

type RouteCandidateStatus struct {
	Provider          string
	Model             string
	Priority          int
	Healthy           bool
	Cooldown          *CooldownState
	RecentLatencyMS   float64 // observation-derived; 0 when unavailable
	RecentSuccessRate float64 // 0-1; 0 when unavailable
}
    RouteCandidateStatus describes a single candidate within a route.

type RouteDecision struct {
	Harness  string
	Provider string
	Model    string
	Reason   string
}
    RouteDecision is the result of ResolveRoute.

type RouteRequest struct {
	Model       string
	Provider    string
	Harness     string
	ModelRef    string
	Reasoning   Reasoning
	Permissions string
}
    RouteRequest specifies a routing query.

type RouteStatusEntry struct {
	Model          string // route key
	Strategy       string // "priority-round-robin" | "first-available" | etc.
	Candidates     []RouteCandidateStatus
	LastDecision   *RouteDecision // most recent ResolveRoute result for this key (cached)
	LastDecisionAt time.Time
}
    RouteStatusEntry describes one configured model route.

type RouteStatusReport struct {
	Routes          []RouteStatusEntry
	GeneratedAt     time.Time
	GlobalCooldowns []CooldownState
}
    RouteStatusReport is returned by RouteStatus.

type ServiceConfig interface {
	// ProviderNames returns provider names in stable order (default first).
	ProviderNames() []string
	// DefaultProviderName returns the name of the configured default provider.
	DefaultProviderName() string
	// Provider returns the raw config values for a named provider.
	Provider(name string) (ServiceProviderEntry, bool)
	// ModelRouteNames returns configured model-route names.
	ModelRouteNames() []string
	// ModelRouteCandidates returns the provider names referenced by a route.
	ModelRouteCandidates(routeName string) []string
	// ModelRouteConfig returns the full route config for a named route.
	// Returns zero value when the route is not found.
	ModelRouteConfig(routeName string) ServiceModelRouteConfig
	// HealthCooldown returns the configured cooldown duration (0 = use default 30s).
	HealthCooldown() time.Duration
	// WorkDir is the base directory for file-backed health state.
	WorkDir() string
}
    ServiceConfig provides provider and routing data to the service without
    creating an import cycle from the root package into agent/config. Callers
    wrap their loaded *config.Config in a type that satisfies this interface.

type ServiceEvent = harnesses.Event
    ServiceEvent is a contract-level event (mirrors harnesses.Event).

type ServiceExecuteRequest struct {
	Prompt       string
	SystemPrompt string
	Model        string
	Provider     string
	Harness      string
	ModelRef     string
	WorkDir      string
	Temperature  float32
	Seed         int64
	Reasoning    Reasoning
	Permissions  string

	// PreResolved bypasses ResolveRoute when the caller already has a
	// decision. When non-nil, agent uses these values verbatim and does
	// not re-route. Provider/Model/Harness fields above are ignored.
	PreResolved *RouteDecision

	// Three independent timeout knobs:
	//   Timeout         — wall-clock cap on the entire request.
	//   IdleTimeout     — streaming-quiet cap; per-stream gap.
	//   ProviderTimeout — per-HTTP-request cap to the provider.
	Timeout         time.Duration
	IdleTimeout     time.Duration
	ProviderTimeout time.Duration

	// Optional stall policy. When non-nil agent enforces and ends with
	// Status="stalled" if any limit hits. Default policy applies when nil.
	StallPolicy *StallPolicy

	// SessionLogDir overrides the default session-log directory for this
	// request (e.g. an execute-bead per-bundle evidence directory).
	SessionLogDir string

	// Metadata is bidirectional: echoed back in every Event AND stamped
	// onto every line of the session log so external consumers correlate.
	Metadata map[string]string
}
    ServiceExecuteRequest is the public ExecuteRequest type per CONTRACT-003.
    See docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md
    (§"Public types") for the canonical shape; this struct is its in-process
    twin under the agent module.

type ServiceModelRouteConfig struct {
	Strategy   string                       // "priority-round-robin" | "ordered-failover" | "smart" | ""
	Candidates []ServiceRouteCandidateEntry // ordered candidate list
}
    ServiceModelRouteConfig carries the routing strategy and candidates for one
    route.

type ServiceOptions struct {
	ConfigPath string    // optional override; default $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ddx-agent/config.yaml
	Logger     io.Writer // optional; agent writes structured session logs internally regardless

	// ServiceConfig, when non-nil, supplies provider and routing data for
	// ListProviders and HealthCheck. Pass a value wrapping the loaded config.
	// When nil, those methods return an error.
	ServiceConfig ServiceConfig
	// Has unexported fields.
}
    ServiceOptions configures a DdxAgent instance.

    seamOptions is embedded so production builds (no testseam tag) get an empty
    struct — making it a compile-time error to set seam fields without the
    build tag. Test builds inherit the four CONTRACT-003 seams (FakeProvider,
    PromptAssertionHook, CompactionAssertionHook, ToolWiringHook) automatically.

type ServiceProviderEndpoint struct {
	Name    string
	BaseURL string
}
    ServiceProviderEndpoint is one configured provider serving endpoint.

type ServiceProviderEntry struct {
	Type      string // "openai" | "openrouter" | "lmstudio" | "omlx" | "ollama" | "anthropic"
	BaseURL   string
	Endpoints []ServiceProviderEndpoint
	APIKey    string
	Model     string // configured default model (may be empty)
}
    ServiceProviderEntry carries the minimal provider data the service needs.

type ServiceRouteCandidateEntry struct {
	Provider string
	Model    string // may be empty (provider default)
	Priority int
}
    ServiceRouteCandidateEntry is one candidate inside a model route.

type StallPolicy struct {
	MaxReadOnlyToolIterations int // 0 = disabled
	MaxNoopCompactions        int // 0 = disabled
}
    StallPolicy bounds how long the agent will spin without making progress
    before terminating with Status="stalled". A nil policy resolves to the
    default in service_execute.go.

     5	
     6	## [Unreleased]
     7	
     8	## [v0.5.0] — 2026-04-19
     9	
    10	### Breaking
    11	- **Removed the legacy `agent.Run` API from the public module surface.**
    12	  The former `Request`, `Result`, `Provider`, `StreamingProvider`, `Tool`,
    13	  `Message`, `ToolDef`, `Event`, session-log DTO, compaction callback, pricing,
    14	  and provider-conformance exports now live behind `internal/` for agent-owned
    15	  code only. External consumers must use `agent.New(...).Execute(...)` and the
    16	  DdxAgent service contract.
    17	- **Removed public compatibility packages for the old provider/tool/session
    18	  stack.** Provider implementations, compaction, prompt building, built-in
    19	  tools, session replay/logging, and provider conformance helpers are no longer
    20	  importable outside this module; Go `internal/` enforcement now blocks
    21	  consumers that have not migrated.
    22	
    23	### Changed
    24	- The standalone `ddx-agent` binary continues to use the internal native loop,
    25	  but that loop is no longer part of the exported Go API.
    26	
    27	### Breaking
    28	- **Removed harness-flavored prompt preset names.** The system prompt preset
    29	  surface now accepts only intent-flavored names: `default`, `smart`, `cheap`,
    30	  `minimal`, and `benchmark`. The old `agent`, `worker`, `cursor`, `claude`,
    31	  and `codex` names now fail with a clear replacement hint instead of
    32	  resolving as aliases. (`.execute-bead-wt-agent-a365bcf2-20260418T023201-36db884d-346c73b4`)
    33	- **Renamed the file-discovery tool from `glob` to `find`.** The built-in tool
    34	  catalog now exposes only `find`; there is no `glob` compatibility alias.
    35	  (`agent-1b00b3ea`)
    36	
    37	## [v0.3.14] — 2026-04-18
    38	
    39	### Fixed
    40	- **Filter SSE comment frames before the `ssestream` decoder.**

   409	
   410	## Reasoning contract
   411	
   412	`Reasoning` is the only preferred public control for model-side reasoning.
   413	Consumers do not set separate public thinking, effort, level, or budget fields.
   414	The scalar accepts named values (`auto`, `off`, `low`, `medium`, `high`) and
   415	provider/harness-supported extended values such as `minimal`, `xhigh` /
   416	`x-high`, and `max`. It also accepts numeric values through
   417	`ReasoningTokens(n)`, where `0` means explicit off and positive integers mean
   418	an explicit max reasoning-token budget or documented provider-equivalent
   419	numeric value.
   420	
   421	Normalization is tri-state:
   422	
   423	- Empty means no caller preference.
   424	- `auto` means resolve model, catalog, or provider defaults.
   425	- `off`, `none`, `false`, and `0` mean explicit reasoning off.
   426	- Positive integers mean an explicit numeric request.
   427	
   428	Default portable named-to-token budgets are `low=2048`, `medium=8192`, and
   429	`high=32768` only when a selected provider/model does not publish a more
   430	specific map. Providers and subprocess harnesses may map resolved reasoning to
   431	wire or CLI knobs named `reasoning`, `thinking`, `effort`, `variant`, or a
   432	numeric budget. They may also drop auto/default reasoning controls for models
   433	that do not support explicit reasoning control. Explicit unsupported values,
   434	unknown extended values, and over-limit numeric values fail clearly.
   435	
   436	Catalog tier defaults are part of route resolution: below-smart tiers
   437	(`cheap`, `fast`, `standard`, `code-economy`, and `code-medium`) default to
   438	`reasoning=off`, including local/economy Qwen targets. `smart` and `code-high`
   439	default to `reasoning=high`. Any explicit caller `Reasoning` value wins over
   440	these defaults, including supported values above high such as `xhigh` or
   441	`max`, and numeric values.
   442	
   443	## Sampling contract
   444	
   445	`ExecuteRequest.Temperature` and `ExecuteRequest.Seed` are the portable
   446	sampling controls. `Temperature=0` requests deterministic sampling. `Seed=0`
   447	means unset and lets the provider choose. Native OpenAI-compatible providers
   448	honor both fields. Providers or subprocess harnesses that do not expose an
   449	equivalent seed control may ignore `Seed`; callers that require strict parity
   450	must treat those runs as advisory/non-deterministic.
   451	
   452	## Bead Execution Policy
   453	
   454	DDx bead implementation should use a two-pass policy against this service:
   455	try a cheap or standard profile with `reasoning=off` first, then escalate only
   456	when the first pass produced evidence that a smarter model is likely to help.
   457	The initial pass should use `ModelRef=cheap` or `ModelRef=standard` with an
   458	explicit `ReasoningOff` request so local/economy models do not spend
   459	reasoning tokens by default.
   460
